closureNotes.outcome: URO Tier 1 T4 overlays landed on EB main at 9c978a6 (PR #310). Per v1.0.0 spec (SR PR #140 20a428f): 18 declared scope files + 3 type-correctness fixes (uro-ws-client.ts + tsconfig adjustments) = 21 net files +2215/-96. Speckle Extensions on ObjectLayers.OVERLAY (VoxelGroupOverlay + ResourcePresenceOverlay with 1s lerp interpolation), click-selection with InstancedMesh instanceId->URN map, display-rules engine (strategy §11 defaults), ResourceDetailPanel side-panel, 4 vitest test files, three@0.149.0 + @types/three@0.149.0 added per DEC-029 LOCKED. All importable from @/lib/uro. Parent arc d-2026-06-05-eb-tier1-ros-overlay-foundation remains S5-in-progress with 4 of 6 children landed (T1 with-debt, T2 clean, T3 with-debt, T4 with-debt). Substrate-correction discipline now fully systemic at authoring layer (T4 author pre-flighted eb-agent:trace-service web-dashboard + DEC-029 three.js version cite + canonical naming throughout); seven new in-execution substrate-corrections surfaced at the type-check/lint/test layer — caught at EB CLI execution, captured in overrideRecord below. Deploy delivery posture: T4 holds at EB main; staging path-filter trigger does NOT match T4 changes; auto-deploy will not fire. Manual build-production-images.yml workflow_dispatch required for staging Watchtower to pick up, OR deploy ladder rework needed (separate scope — new dev-deploy spec d-2026-06-06-eb-deploy-dev-via-pr-label addresses this).

closureNotes.overrideRecord:
Seven in-execution substrate-corrections captured at EB CLI execution (chat-side T4 authoring errors caught at tsc/eslint/vitest before PR merged):

1. tsc invocation pattern. Chat wrapper used bare `tsc --noEmit`; @/ alias only resolves in tsconfig.app.json, so invocation required `--project tsconfig.app.json`. Authoring-layer assumption about default tsconfig; substrate-truth caught at type-check.

2. URN regex too restrictive. display-rules.ts + uro-message-parsers.ts URN_PATTERN authored as `[a-z0-9-]+`; actual canonical IDs are mixed-case (e.g. `steel-beam-A47`, `l2-room-A`). Corrected to `[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9-]*`. Authoring-layer over-restriction; substrate-truth caught at runtime tests.

3. `private enabled` shadowing Speckle Extension base class. VoxelGroupOverlay declared `private enabled: boolean`; Speckle Extension base class exposes `enabled` as a getter/setter. Field declaration shadowed inherited accessor. Removed local declaration. Authoring-layer base-class-API assumption; substrate-truth caught at tsc.

4. MockWebSocket test pattern mismatch. T4 vitest mocks used `addEventListener` but FoxgloveClient uses property-assignment style (`ws.onclose = ...`). Tests rewritten to match FoxgloveClient pattern. Authoring-layer test-pattern assumption; substrate-truth caught at vitest run.

5. SharedArrayBuffer cast required. `data.buffer.slice(...)` returns `ArrayBufferLike` (could be ArrayBuffer OR SharedArrayBuffer); FoxgloveClient APIs accept ArrayBuffer specifically. Added explicit `as ArrayBuffer` cast. Authoring-layer TypeScript narrowing assumption; substrate-truth caught at tsc strict mode.

6. @ts-expect-error scope reduction. T4 authored `@ts-expect-error` blocks covering entire new Set() + add/delete/forEach operations; actually only `new Set()` construction needs the suppression (modern lib.dom types cover the rest). Reduced scope to construction line only. Authoring-layer over-suppression; substrate-truth caught at tsc strict mode.

7. Vitest scope restriction. Initial T4 vitest invocation hit T3 timer-flush test failures (pre-existing, unrelated to T4 scope). Restricted T4 vitest run to src/lib/uro/ + src/components/uro/ directories to isolate T4 validation from T3-pre-existing failures. Memory #26 diff-vs-baseline pattern applied at test layer: T3 failures aren't T4 violations.

Meta-observation across T1→T2→T3→T4 substrate-correction trajectory:
- T1: 1 framework correction (Fastify→Express) — caught at CI, captured at TD
- T2: 0 corrections — T1 lesson absorbed at authoring (eb-agent:trace-service pre-flight)
- T3: 2 corrections (npm version + FU priority) — new anti-patterns at different layers
- T4: 7 corrections at type-check/lint/test layer — none at framework/dependency layer (T1+T3 lessons fully systemic), all at TypeScript strict-mode and base-class API knowledge layer

Pattern improving at higher layers (framework, dependencies, naming) but new anti-patterns continuing to surface at lower layers (type narrowing, base class APIs, test pattern matching). Forward rule for T5 authoring: when extending an existing class hierarchy (Speckle Extension here), enumerate the inherited accessor surface before declaring fields — prevents shadowing-class corrections like (3) above.
